title: FFT Express - BUST ALERT! Players to Avoid in 2025 Fantasy Football! (05/15 Fantasy Football Podcast)
summary: Adam Aizer and Jamey Eisenberg break down the biggest potential busts for the 2025 fantasy football season—based on cost, not talent. This FFT Express episode focuses on players whose current ADPs don't match their expected return on investment. From aging stars like Saquon Barkley and Alvin Kamara to wideouts Mike Evans and Davante Adams, the guys explain why these names could underperform relative to their draft price. title: TOUGH TO RANK! Which RBs and WRs are We Struggling With? (05/14 Fantasy Football Podcast)
summary: The wide receiver position gets tricky beginning as early as late Round 3. There is a big glut at the position that is very tough to rank including guys like DeVonta Smith, Courtland Sutton and Davante Adams, and we'll talk about that at the top of the show. We've also got some news and notes (10:30) on a potential Jameson Williams breakout, the team with the toughest schedule and more ... Let's get to the running backs who are tough to rank beginning with Breece Hall (20:35) and then a trio of RBs (24:40) who could be very good mid-round picks or who could be traps! One of us is very excited about Isiah Pacheco, but there are certainly risks. We also discuss why the Cowboys and Giants RBs (39:00) are tough to rank and then we compare Kaleb Johnson and Quinshon Judkins (41:50) ... On to the WRs (48:00) who are difficult to rank, DJ Moore is the headliner. We also discuss Davante Adams (54:50) and the Chiefs WRs (58:50) ... title: #244: SC Playbook, red flags around Dragons new King
summary: Lyhkan King-Togia is the most traded in player for NRL Supercoach this week, so why are the crew all going against the Dragons playmaker? In this week's edition of the SC Playbook podcast, 2020 NRL Supercoach runner-up Tim Williams, the SC Spy, Bulldogs Chairman Adam Driussi and Matty the Waterboy are on deck ahead of NRL Supercoach round 11. The crew look at team list Tuesday, with Jaxon Purdue's positional shift creating huge interest on the rookie playmaker. They take a look at their bye-period plans around numbers for each week, and work out whether three in-form fullbacks are worth paying up for or going against. 00:00 Adz is back on deck 08:10 Team list Tuesday: Purdue's halves shift 16:00 Crichton's impact on Bulldogs rise 26:30 Bye-planning: How many numbers do we need? 37:00 Fullbacks: Pay up or avoid red-hot trio? 50:30 Hot topics: King-Togia looms as a trap 59:20 Trade and skipper plans 01:04:00 Listener questions: Mahoney the hooking bargain? title: Derek Carr Retires! Plus Five Players We Don't Talk About Enough (05/12 Fantasy Football Podcast)
summary: With Derek Carr retiring, what's next for the Saints (2:40) and what does it mean for Alvin Kamara, Chris Olave and Rashid Shaheed? What do we know about terrible offenses and their ability to produce Fantasy studs? History says that not all hope is lost! ... We focus on five players that we just have not talked about enough! Let's start with Chuba Hubbard (18:00). What's the bust potential for Hubbard? And is De'Von Achane (26:15) an easy first round pick? Will the Dolphins throw to him as much? ... Tony Pollard (37:30) is interesting and the guys seem to think he is a very good value pick, but do they feel that way about Jaylen Waddle (43:50) as he tries to bounce back from a disappointing season? And finally, does Jordan Love (50:00) have the upside that he displayed in 2023? What needs to change for him this season? ... title: Backfield Chaos Rankings: 5 Most Unclear RB Situations! Cowboys, Chiefs, Giants, & More! (05/08 Fantasy Football Podcast)
summary: Heath and Adam dive into the five most chaotic running back situations heading into the 2025 season. They break down the uncertainty in the Giants backfield, where Tyrone Tracy Jr. and Cameron Skattebo are battling for touches. The Chiefs’ crowded RB room is also under the microscope, featuring Isiah Pacheco, Kareem Hunt, and newcomer Elijah Mitchell. Over in New England, can TreVeyon Henderson push Rhamondre Stevenson for lead duties? The Jaguars have a confusing trio of Travis Etienne, Tank Bigsby, and Bhayshul Tuten that could impact fantasy lineups weekly. title: SC ballrs Round 10 - Coming Down & the Origin Crystal Ball
summary: Hello ballrs, Magic Round turned it up a notch with record scoring and ultimate rugby league vibes, but be prepared to come down off that high! Matt (37th), Cmac (24th) and Tubes (14,151st) are here this week to get you ready for Round 10.  Chapters  (00:00) Magic Round Recap  (07:27) Fullback analysis: Kalyn Ponga, James Tedesco, Clint Gutherson, Fletcher Sharpe (18:27) CTW: Jacob Kiraz, Herbie Farnworth, Jaxon Purdue, Sua Fa'alogo (35:57) 5/8 & HFB: King-Togia, AJ Brimson, Nicho Hynes (43:36) 2RF: Corey Horsburgh, Eliesa Katoa, Haumole Olakau'atu, Dylan Lucas, Beau Fermor (50:19) FRF: Luron Patea, Hamish Stewart (57:33) Hooker: AEs, Connor Watson, Cameron McInnes, Jazz Tevaga, Ryley Smith, Sandon Smith (01:07:06) PODs of the Week (01:10:39) Captaincy Chat If you haven’t already, bookmark http://ballr.live so you can see the lightning fast SuperCoach live score updates during games AND whether your player is on or off the field.  Make sure you also get involved in the SC ballrs community, first by joining our unlimited league with league code 730444. The next step is to follow us on our social media channels for plenty more content so you can join the conversation and pose questions to our experts. Then go to http://ballr.promo to get on our mailing list, because there’s some exciting tools coming to help you up your SuperCoach game. title: Round Nine Strategy Session
summary: The fantasy season can turn on one good strategy week. title: Jacob Gibbs's Favorite Values! Rookies Galore! (05/05 Fantasy Football Podcast)
summary: Jacob Gibbs joins our show today with players he is higher on than consensus and this list includes a lot of rookies, but we'll start with Jauan Jennings (3:10) and a look at what the 49ers receiving numbers could look like this season. Then we've got some news and notes and Jacob tells us why RJ Harvey and Kaleb Johnson (11:15) are great fits in Denver and Pittsburgh, respectively ... Let's talk about the Colts wide receivers (27:05)! Jacob makes the case for Josh Downs and Michael Pittman. And we compare those guys to Khalil Shakir (34:10) and Darnell Mooney (42:55). Did Shakir hit his ceiling in 2024, or is there more to come? ... Jacob is also campaigning for a trio of tight ends (44:00), but specifically Colston Loveland. What kind of upside does he see for Loveland in this Ben Johnson offense? ...
